object_type: Euler-Lagrange and metric field equations derived from the closed DFM-MKC action functional V1
purpose: Supply the second closed object in the dark-sector chain: the metric, rigidity-amplitude, and structural-phase field equations formally derived from DFM_MKC_CLOSED_ACTION_FUNCTIONAL_V1.

action_recalled.lagrangian_density: L_total = (R - 2 Lambda)/(16 pi G) - (alpha/2) g^{mu nu} nabla_mu phi nabla_nu phi - (beta/2) phi^2 g^{mu nu} nabla_mu theta nabla_nu theta - U(phi) + L_vis(psi_vis, g)
action_recalled.potential: U(phi) = rho_star + (1/2) m_phi_squared phi^2 + (1/4) lambda_phi phi^4

metric_equation.symbolic_statement: G_{mu nu} + Lambda g_{mu nu} = 8 pi G (T_vis_{mu nu} + T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu})
metric_equation.einstein_tensor: G_{mu nu} = R_{mu nu} - (1/2) R g_{mu nu}
metric_equation.visible_stress_energy: T_vis_{mu nu} = -(2/sqrt(-g)) delta(sqrt(-g) L_vis)/delta g^{mu nu}
metric_equation.dark_sector_stress_energy: T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu} = alpha nabla_mu phi nabla_nu phi + beta phi^2 nabla_mu theta nabla_nu theta - g_{mu nu}[(alpha/2)(nabla phi)^2 + (beta/2) phi^2 (nabla theta)^2 + U(phi)]

dark_sector_equations.rigidity_amplitude_equation: alpha Box_g phi - beta phi (nabla theta)^2 - U_prime(phi) = 0

definitions.(nabla phi)^2: g^{mu nu} nabla_mu phi nabla_nu phi
definitions.(nabla theta)^2: g^{mu nu} nabla_mu theta nabla_nu theta

dark_sector_equations.periodic_phase_status: theta is interpreted modulo 2 pi; local field equation is written on a branch chart.

constraint_equations.hamiltonian_momentum_constraints_status: Available after choosing a 3+1 foliation; not expanded in this covariant V1 object.
constraint_equations.covariant_constraint: nabla^mu [T_vis_{mu nu} + T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu}] = 0 follows from diffeomorphism invariance when the field equations hold.
constraint_equations.phase_current_conservation: J_theta^mu = beta phi^2 nabla^mu theta satisfies nabla_mu J_theta^mu = 0.

conservation_laws.visible_sector_exchange_status: In V1 minimal coupling, visible matter couples through g only; direct non-gravitational exchange with phi or theta is not introduced.

stress_energy_tensor.dark_sector_tensor: T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu} = alpha nabla_mu phi nabla_nu phi + beta phi^2 nabla_mu theta nabla_nu theta - g_{mu nu}[(alpha/2)(nabla phi)^2 + (beta/2) phi^2 (nabla theta)^2 + U(phi)]
stress_energy_tensor.energy_density_in_local_inertial_frame: rho_DFM_MKC = (alpha/2)[(partial_t phi)^2 + |grad phi|^2] + (beta/2) phi^2[(partial_t theta)^2 + |grad theta|^2] + U(phi), subject to the chosen local frame convention
stress_energy_tensor.pressure_anisotropy_status: Possible when spatial gradients of phi or theta are nonzero.
stress_energy_tensor.perfect_fluid_reduction_status: Requires homogeneous/isotropic specialization and is deferred to later cosmology objects.

gauge_or_coordinate_conditions.covariant_form: No coordinate gauge is fixed in the V1 covariant equations.
gauge_or_coordinate_conditions.well_posedness_status: Classical well-posedness is not proved here; standard hyperbolic gauge choices are required for PDE analysis.

known_limit_recovery.limit: phi = 0 and rho_star absorbed into Lambda
known_limit_recovery.recovered_equations: Einstein equations with visible stress-energy and effective cosmological constant.
Einstein gravity coupled to one real scalar phi with potential U(phi).
Flat-spacetime amplitude equation alpha Box phi - beta phi (partial theta)^2 - U_prime(phi) = 0 and phase-current equation partial_mu(beta phi^2 partial^mu theta) = 0.
No propagating DFM-MKC field equations remain; the dark sector contributes only a vacuum-energy shift.

does_not_prove: DFM-MKC ACT Planck DESI prediction vector
next_admissible_step: Supply DFM_MKC_MATTER_COUPLING_RULE_V1 as the explicit visible-sector and photon coupling interface.
